## Summary

<!-- What does this PR change and why? -->

**Base branch:** <!-- e.g. `main` for hotfix, or `rc/0.2.0` for release-train features -->

## Title (required for squash merge)

Use a **Conventional Commits** title — it becomes the merge commit on `main` and **feeds automated semver + CHANGELOG** (e.g. `feat(daemon): add stdio transport`, `fix: reject path traversal`).

## Type

- [ ] `feat` — new feature or behavior
- [ ] `fix` — bug fix
- [ ] `docs` — documentation only
- [ ] `chore` — tooling, CI, deps, refactor with no user-visible change

## Checklist

- [ ] **Branch name** matches the work (`feat/…`, `fix/…`, `docs/…`, etc.) — see CONTRIBUTING.md
- [ ] **Docusaurus / docs** updated if the change is user- or operator-visible (or note “N/A—internal”)
- [ ] **Incremental commits** where practical (reversible history)
- [ ] **Copilot / code review** addressed or explicitly discussed before merge
- [ ] I am **not** stacking unrelated future work in this PR; next work waits for merge to `main` (see team workflow)
